correct_model.comment: Total cost = ADDITIVE rules only. The never rule contributes 0 and REDUCES the always rule's net (it removes the uid=0 execve slice). Net cost <= the always rule alone.
correct_model.math: always execve ~1200 B/event. typical 50000*1200/1e9 = 0.06 GB/day; 0.06*30.4*5.00 = $9.12/mo. The never rule subtracts the suppressed uid=0 portion (workload-dependent, f3 §5), so the TRUE net is <= $9.12/mo typical.

naive_wrong_model.comment: A naive impl that counts the never rule as ADDITIVE HIGH execve volume DOUBLE-counts execve volume.
naive_wrong_model.error: ~2x overstatement (the never rule's HIGH band added on top of the always rule's). The correct answer is <= the single always rule; the never rule never adds.
